features.name: Frenzy
features.description: If you use Reckless Attack while your Rage is active, you deal extra damage to the first target you hit on your turn with a Strength-based attack. To determine the extra damage, roll a number of d6s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. The damage has the same type as the weapon or Unarmed Strike used for the attack.

features.name: Mindless Rage
features.description: You have Immunity to the Charmed and Frightened conditions while your Rage is active. If you're Charmed or Frightened when you enter your Rage, the condition ends on you.

features.name: Retaliation
features.description: When you take damage from a creature that is within 5 feet of you, you can take a Reaction to make one melee attack against that creature, using a weapon or an Unarmed Strike.

features.name: Intimidating Presence
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can strike terror into others with your menacing presence and primal power. When you do so, each creature of your choice in a 30-foot Emanation originating from you must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, a creature has the Frightened condition for 1 minute. At the end of each of the Frightened creature's turns, the creature repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest unless you expend a use of your Rage (no action required) to restore your use of it.

features.name: Animal Instincts
features.description: Your attunement to the wild lets you notice things that others might miss. You gain proficiency in two of the following skills: Animal Handling, Medicine, Nature, Perception, Stealth, or Survival.

features.name: Speak with Animals
features.description: You gain the ability to speak with animals, and they understand you as if you shared a common language.

features.name: Spirit Walk
features.description: You can cast the Commune with Nature spell, but only as a ritual. When you do so, you can choose one of the following benefits: Bear (resistance to all damage except psychic), Eagle (gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed), Wolf (your allies gain advantage on attack rolls against creatures within 5 feet of you).

features.name: Bestial Soul
features.description: Your connection to the wild transforms your body. You gain one of the following benefits: Bear (you gain resistance to all damage except psychic), Eagle (you gain a flying speed of 30 feet), Wolf (you can take the Dash or Disengage action as a bonus action).

features.name: Trample the Fallen
features.description: Creatures you knock prone with a melee attack have disadvantage on the saving throw against your Prone DC until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Vitality of the Tree
features.description: Your rage gives you vitality. While raging, you gain temporary hit points at the start of each of your turns. The number of temporary hit points equals 2 + your Barbarian level.

features.name: Branches of the Tree
features.description: You can use your bonus action to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you can see. Alternatively, you can choose to become the target of an attack in place of a willing creature within 5 feet of you.

features.name: Bark Like Iron
features.description: Your skin hardens like bark. While raging, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.

features.name: Travel Along the Tree
features.description: You can cast the Tree Stride spell, but only as a ritual. When you do so, you can bring up to three willing creatures you can see within 5 feet of you with you.

features.name: Roots of the Tree
features.description: When you use your Branches of the Tree, you can choose to gain the benefits of the Haste spell for 1 minute. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Divine Fury
features.description: The zealot's rage comes from a divine source. While raging, the first time on each of your turns that you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can deal extra damage equal to 1d6 + your Barbarian level.

features.name: Warrior of the Gods
features.description: At 6th level, you gain the ability to call on the gods to aid you. As a bonus action, you can choose to end one condition or effect on yourself that is caused by a spell.

features.name: Zealous Presence
features.description: As a bonus action, you exude magical menace. Until the end of your next turn, you and your allies within 30 feet of you have advantage on attack rolls and saving throws.

features.name: Rage of the Zealot
features.description: While raging, you are immune to the charmed and frightened conditions. Additionally, when you make a death saving throw, you can choose to succeed instead.

features.name: Well-Versed
features.description: You are a prodigy of the dance. You gain proficiency in Performance and one other skill of your choice.

features.name: Dance Moves
features.description: You learn three dance moves of your choice from the Dance Moves list. While you are wearing light or no armor, you can use your bonus action to perform one of your dance moves.

features.name: Reactive Movement
features.description: When a creature misses you with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to move up to half your speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

features.name: Irresistible Dance
features.description: You can cast the Otto's Irresistible Dance spell without components. Once you do so, you can't cast it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Saint of Dancers
features.description: Your movement is unparalleled. You can take the Dash or Disengage action as a bonus action, and you can move through the space of any creature that is at least two sizes larger than you.

features.name: Mantle of Inspiration
features.description: You can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to grant yourself and up to five other creatures within 60 feet of you temporary hit points equal to 2d6 + your Charisma modifier.

features.name: Enthralling Performance
features.description: You can cast Charm Person without components. You can cast it as a bonus action, and it doesn't count against your spells known.

features.name: Mantle of Majesty
features.description: You can cast the Command spell without components. You can cast it as a bonus action, and it doesn't count against your spells known.

features.name: Unbreakable Majesty
features.description: As a bonus action, you can assume an unbreakable majesty for 1 minute. For the duration, any creature that hits you with an attack roll must first succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be banished to a harmless demiplane.

features.name: Glamorous Presence
features.description: You can cast the Greater Invisibility spell without components. Once you do so, you can't cast it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with three skills of your choice.

features.name: Cutting Words
features.description: You learn to use your wit to distract, confuse, and otherwise sap the confidence and competence of others. When a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you makes an attack roll, an ability check, or a damage roll, you can use your reaction to expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration, rolling a Bardic Inspiration die and subtracting the number rolled from the creature's roll.

features.name: Magical Discoveries
features.description: You learn two spells of your choice. These spells can come from the Cleric, Druid, or Wizard spell list or any combination thereof. A spell you choose must be a cantrip or a spell for which you have spell slots. You always have the chosen spells prepared, and whenever you gain a Bard level, you can replace one of the spells with another spell that meets these requirements.

features.name: Magical Secrets
features.description: You learn two additional spells of your choice from the Bard, Cleric, Druid, and Wizard spell lists. The chosen spells count as Bard spells for you.

features.name: Peerless Skill
features.description: When you make an ability check or attack roll and fail, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration; roll the Bardic Inspiration die, and add the number rolled to the d20, potentially turning a failure into a success. On a failure, the Bardic Inspiration isn't expended.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: You gain proficiency with three skills of your choice.

features.name: Cutting Words
features.description: You learn to use your wit to supernaturally distract, confuse, and otherwise sap the confidence and competence of others. When a creature that you can see within 60 feet of yourself makes a damage roll or succeeds on an ability check or attack roll, you can take a Reaction to expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration; roll your Bardic Inspiration die, and subtract the number rolled from the creature's roll, reducing the damage or potentially turning the success into a failure.

features.name: Magical Discoveries
features.description: You learn two spells of your choice. These spells can come from the Cleric, Druid, or Wizard spell list or any combination thereof (see a class's section for its spell list). A spell you choose must be a cantrip or a spell for which you have spell slots, as shown in the Bard Features table. You always have the chosen spells prepared, and whenever you gain a Bard level, you can replace one of the spells with another spell that meets these requirements.

features.name: Peerless Skill
features.description: When you make an ability check or attack roll and fail, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration; roll the Bardic Inspiration die, and add the number rolled to the d20, potentially turning a failure into a success. On a failure, the Bardic Inspiration isn't expended.

features.name: Combat Inspiration
features.description: You learn to inspire others in battle. When a creature uses a Bardic Inspiration die, it can roll the die and add the number rolled to a weapon damage roll it just made.

features.name: Battle Magic
features.description: You can cast a bard spell and make a weapon attack in the same action.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Battle Magic Improved
features.description: When you cast a bard spell with a casting time of 1 action, you can make two weapon attacks as a bonus action.

features.name: Inspiring Surge
features.description: When you use your action to cast a bard spell, you can use your bonus action to encourage one creature within 60 feet of you. That creature can use its reaction to make one weapon attack or take the Dash action.

features.name: Disciple of Life
features.description: When a spell you cast with a spell slot restores Hit Points to a creature, that creature regains additional Hit Points on the turn you cast the spell. The additional Hit Points equal 2 plus the spell slot's level.

features.name: Life Domain Spells
features.description: Your connection to this divine domain ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the Life Domain Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. Table: Life Domain Spells | Cleric Level    | Prepared Spells                                | |-----------------|------------------------------------------------| | 3               | Aid, Bless, Cure Wounds, Lesser Restoration    | | 5               | Mass Healing Word, Revivify                    | | 7               | Aura of Life, Death Ward                       | | 9               | Greater Restoration, Mass Cure Wounds          |

features.name: Preserve Life
features.description: As a Magic action, you present your Holy Symbol and expend a use of your Channel Divinity to evoke healing energy that can restore a number of Hit Points equal to five times your Cleric level. Choose Bloodied creatures within 30 feet of yourself (which can include you), and divide those Hit Points among them. This feature can restore a creature to no more than half its Hit Point maximum.

features.name: Blessed Healer
features.description: The healing spells you cast on others heal you as well. Immediately after you cast a spell with a spell slot that restores Hit Points to one or more creatures other than yourself, you regain Hit Points equal to 2 plus the spell slot's level.

features.name: Supreme Healing
features.description: When you would normally roll one or more dice to restore Hit Points to a creature with a spell or Channel Divinity, don't roll those dice for the healing; instead use the highest number possible for each die. For example, instead of restoring 2d6 Hit Points to a creature with a spell, you restore 12.

features.name: Warding Flare
features.description: You can interpose divine light between yourself and an attacking enemy. When you are attacked by a creature within 30 feet of you that you can see, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Radiance
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to unleash a beam of radiance. As an action, you present your holy symbol and cause a beam of light to strike a creature within 60 feet of you. The creature must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 3d10 + your cleric level in radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

features.name: Improved Flare
features.description: You can now use Warding Flare a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 fire damage to the target.

features.name: Corona of Light
features.description: You can use your action to activate an aura of sunlight that lasts for 1 minute or until you dismiss it using another action. While active, the aura emits bright light in a 60-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Enemies in the bright light have disadvantage on saving throws against your cleric spells.

features.name: Blessing of the Trickster
features.description: You can grant another creature advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. This blessing lasts for 1 hour or until you use it on another creature.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Invoke Duplicity
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to create a perfect illusion of yourself. As an action, you create a perfect copy of yourself in an unoccupied space that you can see within 30 feet of you. The copy lasts for 1 minute or until you dismiss it as an action.

features.name: Cloak of Shadows
features.description: You can use your action to become invisible until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 poison damage to the target.

features.name: Improved Duplicity
features.description: You can use your Invoke Duplicity a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: War Priest
features.description: You can interpose divine power between yourself and an attack. When you are attacked by a creature within 30 feet of you that you can see, you can use your reaction to add your Wisdom modifier to your AC against that attack.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Guided Strike
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to gain a +10 bonus to an attack roll you make. You make this choice after you see the roll, but before the DM says whether the attack hits or misses.

features.name: Cloak of Shadows
features.description: You gain the ability to cloak yourself in divine shadows. You can use your action to become invisible until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Divine Strike
features.description: Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 damage to the target.

features.name: Avatar of Battle
features.description: You gain resistance to nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

features.name: Circle of the Land Spells
features.description: Whenever you finish a Long Rest, choose one type of land: arid, polar, temperate, or tropical. Consult the table below that corresponds to the chosen type; you have the spells listed for your Druid level and lower prepared. Table: Arid Land | Druid Level | Circle Spells                  | |-------------|--------------------------------| | 3           | Blur, Burning Hands, Fire Bolt | | 5           | Fireball                       | | 7           | Blight                         | | 9           | Wall of Stone                  | Table: Polar Land | Druid Level | Circle Spells                        | |-------------|--------------------------------------| | 3           | Fog Cloud, Hold Person, Ray of Frost | | 5           | Sleet Storm                          | | 7           | Ice Storm                            | | 9           | Cone of Cold                         | Table: Temperate Land | Druid Level | Circle Spells                     | |-------------|-----------------------------------| | 3           | Misty Step, Shocking Grasp, Sleep | | 5           | Lightning Bolt                    | | 7           | Freedom of Movement               | | 9           | Tree Stride                       | Table: Tropical Land | Druid Level | Circle Spells                     | |-------------|-----------------------------------| | 3           | Acid Splash, Ray of Sickness, Web | | 5           | Stinking Cloud                    | | 7           | Polymorph                         | | 9           | Insect Plague                     |

features.name: Land's Aid
features.description: As a Magic action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape and choose a point within 60 feet of yourself. Vitality-giving flowers and life-draining thorns appear for a moment in a 10-foot-radius Sphere centered on that point. Each creature of your choice in the Sphere must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC, taking 2d6 Necrotic damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. One creature of your choice in that area regains 2d6 Hit Points. The damage and healing increase by 1d6 when you reach Druid levels 10 (3d6) and 14 (4d6).

features.name: Natural Recovery
features.description: You can cast one of the level 1+ spells that you have prepared from your Circle Spells feature without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a Long Rest before you do so again. In addition, when you finish a Short Rest, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level that is equal to or less than half your Druid level (round up), and none of them can be level 6+. For example, if you're a level 6 Druid, you can recover up to three levels' worth of spell slots. You can recover a level 3 spell slot, a level 2 and a level 1 spell slot, or three level 1 spell slots. Once you recover spell slots with this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Nature's Ward
features.description: You are immune to the Poisoned condition, and you have Resistance to a damage type associated with your current land choice in the Circle Spells feature, as shown in the Nature's Ward table. Table: Nature's Ward | Land Type | Resistance | Land Type | Resistance | |-----------|------------|-----------|------------| | Arid      | Fire       | Temperate | Lightning  | | Polar     | Cold       | Tropical  | Poison     |

features.name: Nature's Sanctuary
features.description: As a Magic action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape and cause spectral trees and vines to appear in a 15-foot Cube on the ground within 120 feet of yourself. They last there for 1 minute or until you have the Incapacitated condition or die. You and your allies have Half Cover while in that area, and your allies gain the current Resistance of your Nature's Ward while there. As a Bonus Action, you can move the Cube up to 60 feet to ground within 120 feet of yourself.

features.name: Combat Wild Shape
features.description: You can use your Wild Shape to transform into a beast with a challenge rating as high as 1. When you use Wild Shape to transform, you can choose to regain a number of hit points equal to half your druid level (rounded up).

features.name: Circle Forms
features.description: You can transform into a beast with a challenge rating as high as your proficiency bonus.

features.name: Moonlight Step
features.description: You can use your bonus action to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you can see. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Improved Combat Wild Shape
features.description: When you use your Wild Shape to transform, you can choose to regain all your hit points. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Sea Swell
features.description: You can use your bonus action to cause a swell of water to rise up beneath you. You gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed and can breathe underwater.

features.name: Ocean's Grace
features.description: You gain resistance to cold damage, and you can speak with beasts and plants that dwell in the sea.

features.name: Oceanic Soul
features.description: You can cast the Control Water spell without components. Once you do so, you can't cast it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Tsunami
features.description: As an action, you can summon a massive wave that crashes down on your enemies. Each creature in a 60-foot cone must make a Strength saving throw, taking 8d10 bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

features.name: Star Map
features.description: You learn to use a star map as your spellcasting focus. You gain the Guidance cantrip, and you can cast it as a bonus action.

features.name: Starry Form
features.description: As a bonus action, you can expend a use of your Wild Shape to take on the shape of a constellation. While in this form, you gain a flying speed of 20 feet and can hover, and you have resistance to cold and fire damage.

features.name: Cosmic Omen
features.description: You can cast the Augury or Divination spell without components. Once you cast either spell in this way, you can't cast it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Twinkling Constellations
features.description: While in Starry Form, you can choose to gain the benefits of one of the following constellations: Archer (ranged spell attacks), Chalice (healing), Dragon (breath weapon), Form of Dread (frighten enemies), Giant (throw rocks), Sea (swimming and breathing underwater), Shepherd (summon nature's ally), Tundra (cold damage).

features.name: Full of Stars
features.description: While in Starry Form, you have a flying speed of 40 feet and can hover. Additionally, you can use your action to unleash a burst of stellar energy, forcing each creature within 30 feet of you to make a Dexterity saving throw or take 4d10 radiant damage.

features.name: Combat Superiority
features.description: You learn maneuvers that are fueled by special dice called superiority dice. You gain three maneuvers of your choice from the list of available maneuvers.

features.name: Student of War
features.description: You gain proficiency with one skill of your choice.

features.name: Know Your Enemy
features.description: If you spend at least 1 minute observing or interacting with another creature outside combat, you can learn certain information about its capabilities compared to your own.

features.name: Improved Combat Superiority
features.description: Your superiority dice turn into d10s at 10th level, and d12s at 18th level.

features.name: Relentless
features.description: Starting at 15th level, when you roll initiative and have no superiority dice remaining, you regain 1 superiority die.

features.name: Improved Critical
features.description: Your attack rolls with weapons and Unarmed Strikes can score a Critical Hit on a roll of 19 or 20 on the d20.

features.name: Remarkable Athlete
features.description: Thanks to your athleticism, you have Advantage on Initiative rolls and Strength (Athletics) checks. In addition, immediately after you score a Critical Hit, you can move up to half your Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

features.name: Additional Fighting Style
features.description: You gain another Fighting Style feat of your choice.

features.name: Heroic Warrior
features.description: The thrill of battle drives you toward victory. During combat, you can give yourself Heroic Inspiration whenever you start your turn without it.

features.name: Superior Critical
features.description: Your attack rolls with weapons and Unarmed Strikes can now score a Critical Hit on a roll of 18–20 on the d20.

features.name: Survivor
features.description: You attain the pinnacle of resilience in battle, giving you these benefits. **Defy Death.** You have Advantage on Death Saving Throws. Moreover, when you roll 18–20 on a Death Saving Throw, you gain the benefit of rolling a 20 on it. **Heroic Rally.** At the start of each of your turns, you regain Hit Points equal to 5 plus your Constitution modifier if you are Bloodied and have at least 1 Hit Point.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You know three 1st-level wizard spells of your choice, two of which you must choose from the evocation and abjuration spells on the wizard spell list.

features.name: Weapon Bond
features.description: You can use a bonus action to magically bond with one weapon. While bonded, you can't be disarmed of that weapon unless you are incapacitated.

features.name: War Magic
features.description: You can use your bonus action to make a weapon attack after you cast a cantrip.

features.name: Eldritch Strike
features.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, that creature has disadvantage on the next saving throw it makes against a spell you cast before the end of your next turn.

features.name: Arcane Charge
features.description: You can use your action to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you can see, and you can use your action to make a weapon attack against a creature within range.

features.name: Psionic Power
features.description: You harbor a wellspring of psionic energy that can alter your physical form. You gain a number of Psionic Energy dice equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Telekinetic Adept
features.description: You gain the Telekinesis spell, which you can cast without components. Once you cast it in this way, you can't cast it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Psionic Strike
features.description: You can use your psionic energy to augment your weapon attacks. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can expend one Psionic Energy die and roll it, adding the number rolled to the attack's damage.

features.name: Telekinetic Master
features.description: You can cast the Telekinesis spell as a bonus action, and you can use your action to make a weapon attack or shove a creature with your telekinesis.

features.name: Ascendant Mind
features.description: You can use your psionic energy to fly. As a bonus action, you gain a flying speed of 30 feet until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Hands of Healing
features.description: Your meditative focus and gestures can restore vitality. As an action, you can restore hit points equal to a roll of your Martial Arts die + your Wisdom modifier to another creature that you can see within 5 feet of you.

features.name: Hands of Harm
features.description: You can use your ki to inflict agony on others. When you hit a creature with an unarmed strike, you can spend 1 ki point to deal extra damage equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die.

features.name: Physician's Touch
features.description: You can use your action to attempt to cure one disease or neutralize one poison afflicting a creature that you can touch.

features.name: Flurry of Healing and Harm
features.description: When you use Flurry of Blows, you can use your bonus action to either restore hit points to another creature within 5 feet of you or to deal extra damage to a creature you hit with an unarmed strike.

features.name: Hand of Ultimate Mercy
features.description: You can use your action to touch the corpse of a creature that died within the past hour. That creature returns to life with 1 hit point.

features.name: Shadow Arts
features.description: You can use your ki to become one with the shadows. As a bonus action, you can spend 1 ki point to become invisible until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Shadow Step
features.description: You can use your bonus action to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you can see that is in dim light or darkness.

features.name: Cloak of Shadows
features.description: You can use your action to become invisible until the end of your next turn. While invisible in this way, you can't be detected by any means except a creature using Detect Magic or Truesight.

features.name: Shadow Warrior
features.description: You can use your action to become a shadow of yourself. For 1 minute, you gain the following benefits: resistance to all damage except psychic, advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks, and you can move through other creatures' spaces as if they were difficult terrain.

features.name: Elemental Attunement
features.description: You can use your ki to harness the elements. As a bonus action, you can spend 1 ki point to gain one of the following benefits for 1 minute: Fangs of the Fire Snake (fire damage), Shape of the Flowing River (water damage), Sweeping Cinder Strike (fire damage), Water Whip (pull creature), Wheel of Fire and Ice (cold and fire damage).

features.name: Elemental Burst
features.description: When you use your Elemental Attunement, you can choose to expend additional ki points to unleash an elemental burst. The burst deals damage to all creatures within 10 feet of you.

features.name: Elemental Mastery
features.description: You can cast the Elementalism spell without components. Once you do so, you can't cast it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Perfect Elements
features.description: Your attunement to the elements is complete. You can use your action to become a being of pure elemental energy for 1 minute. While in this form, you have resistance to all damage except psychic, and you can move through other creatures' spaces as if they were difficult terrain.

features.name: Open Hand Technique
features.description: Whenever you hit a creature with an attack granted by your Flurry of Blows, you can impose one of the following effects on that target. **Addle.** The target can't make Opportunity Attacks until the start of its next turn. **Push.** The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 15 feet away from you. **Topple.** The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or have the Prone condition.

features.name: Wholeness of Body
features.description: You gain the ability to heal yourself. As a Bonus Action, you can roll your Martial Arts die. You regain a number of Hit Points equal to the number rolled plus your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 Hit Point regained). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Fleet Step
features.description: When you take a Bonus Action other than Step of the Wind, you can also use Step of the Wind immediately after that Bonus Action.

features.name: Quivering Palm
features.description: You gain the ability to set up lethal vibrations in someone's body. When you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike, you can expend 4 Focus Points to start these imperceptible vibrations, which last for a number of days equal to your Monk level. The vibrations are harmless unless you take an action to end them. Alternatively, when you take the Attack action on your turn, you can forgo one of the attacks to end the vibrations. To end them, you and the target must be on the same plane of existence. When you end them, the target must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 10d12 Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. You can have only one creature under the effect of this feature at a time. You can end the vibrations harmlessly (no action required).

features.name: Oath of Devotion Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Devotion Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. Table: Oath of Devotion Spells | Paladin Level | Spells                                            | |---------------|---------------------------------------------------| | 3             | Protection from Evil and Good, Shield of Faith    | | 5             | Aid, Zone of Truth                                | | 9             | Beacon of Hope, Dispel Magic                      | | 13            | Freedom of Movement, Guardian of Faith            | | 17            | Commune, Flame Strike                             |

features.name: Sacred Weapon
features.description: When you take the Attack action, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to imbue one Melee weapon that you are holding with positive energy. For 10 minutes or until you use this feature again, you add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls you make with that weapon (minimum bonus of +1), and each time you hit with it, you cause it to deal its normal damage type or Radiant damage. The weapon also emits Bright Light in a 20-foot radius and Dim Light 20 feet beyond that. You can end this effect early (no action required). This effect also ends if you aren't carrying the weapon.

features.name: Aura of Devotion
features.description: You and your allies have Immunity to the Charmed condition while in your Aura of Protection. If a Charmed ally enters the aura, that condition has no effect on that ally while there.

features.name: Smite of Protection
features.description: Your magical smite now radiates protective energy. Whenever you cast *Divine Smite*, you and your allies have Half Cover while in your Aura of Protection. The aura has this benefit until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Holy Nimbus
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can imbue your Aura of Protection with holy power, granting the benefits below for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5 spell slot (no action required). **Holy Ward.** You have Advantage on any saving throw you are forced to make by a Fiend or an Undead. **Radiant Damage.** Whenever an enemy starts its turn in the aura, that creature takes Radiant damage equal to your Charisma modifier plus your Proficiency Bonus. **Sunlight.** The aura is filled with Bright Light that is sunlight.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Peerless Athlete
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to augment your athleticism. As a bonus action, you can touch one creature and give them advantage on their next Strength (Athletics) or Dexterity (Acrobatics) check.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Inspiring Smite
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to turn a smite into a moment of inspiration. When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can expend one use of your Channel Divinity to cause the attack to deal an extra 1d8 radiant damage, and you can choose to give one creature you can see within 30 feet of you a Bardic Inspiration die.

features.name: Aura of Alacrity
features.description: You and your allies within 10 feet of you gain a +10 bonus to their walking speed. At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

features.name: Glorious Defense
features.description: You can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll of a creature that attacks you or an ally within 10 feet of you. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Living Legend
features.description: As an action, you can become a living legend for 1 minute. For the duration, you gain the following benefits: you are immune to the charmed and frightened conditions, attack rolls against you have disadvantage, and when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can choose to deal an extra 3d8 radiant damage to the target.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Nature's Wrath
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to invoke the power of nature. As an action, you can cause spectral vines to sprout from the ground beneath a creature within 10 feet of you that you can see. The creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw or become restrained for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Turn the Faithless
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to make fey and fiends flee. As an action, you present your holy symbol and speak a prayer. Each fey or fiend within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is turned for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

features.name: Aura of Warding
features.description: You and your allies within 10 feet of you have resistance to spell damage. At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

features.name: Undying Sentinel
features.description: You can use your action to regain hit points equal to half your paladin level (rounded up). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Eldritch Strike
features.description: As an action, you can attack a creature with a melee weapon attack. If the attack hits, the target takes an extra 4d10 force damage, and you can choose to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you can see.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Abjure Enemy
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to abjure an enemy. As an action, you present your holy symbol and speak a prayer of denunciation. Choose one creature within 60 feet of you that you can see. That creature must make a Wisdom saving throw, becoming frightened for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

features.name: Channel Divinity: Vow of Enmity
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to make an enemy your sworn foe. As a bonus action, you can choose one creature within 10 feet of you that you can see. You gain advantage on attack rolls against that creature for 1 minute or until it drops to 0 hit points.

features.name: Relentless Avenger
features.description: When a creature hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to move up to half your speed without provoking opportunity attacks. If you end your movement within 5 feet of the attacker, you can make one melee weapon attack against it.

features.name: Soul of Vengeance
features.description: When a creature within 10 feet of you hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature.

features.name: Avenging Angel
features.description: As an action, you can transform into an avenging angel for 1 hour. For the duration, you gain the following benefits: you have a flying speed of 60 feet, you emit bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet, and attack rolls against you have disadvantage.

features.name: Ranger's Companion
features.description: You form a mystical bond with a beast. Choose a beast that is no larger than Medium and has a challenge rating of 1/4 or lower. The beast obeys your commands and gains a special bond with you.

features.name: Exceptional Training
features.description: Your companion has grown in power. It gains the benefit of your Favored Enemy feature, and it adds your proficiency bonus to its AC and attack rolls.

features.name: Bestial Fury
features.description: Your companion can use its action to make two attacks when it takes the Attack action.

features.name: Share Spells
features.description: When you cast a spell that targets only you, you can also affect your companion with the spell if it is within 30 feet of you.

features.name: Fey Wanderer Magic
features.description: You learn the Charm Person and Misty Step spells. You can cast Charm Person without components, and you can cast it as a bonus action.

features.name: Fey Step
features.description: As a bonus action, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you can see. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Fey Reinforcements
features.description: You can cast the Summon Fey spell without components. Once you do so, you can't cast it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Misty Wanderer
features.description: You can cast the Misty Step spell without components, and you can cast it as a bonus action.

features.name: Feywild Gift
features.description: You can use your action to grant yourself and your allies within 30 feet of you advantage on the next attack roll or saving throw each of them makes before the start of your next turn.

features.name: Dread Ambusher
features.description: You can take the Attack action on the first round of combat as a bonus action. You can make one additional weapon attack as part of that action.

features.name: Umbral Sight
features.description: You gain darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have darkvision, its range increases by 30 feet.

features.name: Iron Mind
features.description: You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws.

features.name: Stalker's Flurry
features.description: When you miss with a weapon attack on your turn, you can make an additional weapon attack as a bonus action.

features.name: Shadowy Dodge
features.description: When a creature makes an attack roll against you and you can see the attacker, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Hunter's Lore
features.description: You can call on the forces of nature to reveal certain strengths and weaknesses of your prey. While a creature is marked by your *Hunter's Mark*, you know whether that creature has any Immunities, Resistances, or Vulnerabilities, and if the creature has any, you know what they are.

features.name: Hunter's Prey
features.description: You gain one of the following feature options of your choice. Whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest, you can replace the chosen option with the other one. **Colossus Slayer.** Your tenacity can wear down even the most resilient foes. When you hit a creature with a weapon, the weapon deals an extra 1d8 damage to the target if it's missing any of its Hit Points. You can deal this extra damage only once per turn. **Horde Breaker.** Once on each of your turns when you make an attack with a weapon, you can make another attack with the same weapon against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target, that is within the weapon's range, and that you haven't attacked this turn.

features.name: Defensive Tactics
features.description: You gain one of the following feature options of your choice. Whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest, you can replace the chosen option with the other one. **Escape the Horde.** Opportunity Attacks have Disadvantage against you. **Multiattack Defense.** When a creature hits you with an attack roll, that creature has Disadvantage on all other attack rolls against you this turn.

features.name: Superior Hunter's Prey
features.description: Once per turn when you deal damage to a creature marked by your *Hunter's Mark*, you can also deal that spell's extra damage to a different creature that you can see within 30 feet of the first creature.

features.name: Superior Hunter's Defense
features.description: When you take damage, you can take a Reaction to give yourself Resistance to that damage and any other damage of the same type until the end of the current turn.

features.name: Spellcasting
features.description: You know three 1st-level wizard spells of your choice, two of which you must choose from the enchantment and illusion spells on the wizard spell list.

features.name: Mage Hand Legerdemain
features.description: When you cast Mage Hand, you can make the hand invisible, and you can use your bonus action to make the hand perform one of the following tasks: steal an object, stow an object, retrieve an object.

features.name: Magical Ambush
features.description: If you are hidden from a creature when you cast a spell on it, the creature has disadvantage on any saving throw it makes against the spell.

features.name: Versatile Trickster
features.description: You can use your bonus action to command your Mage Hand to distract a creature within 5 feet of it. The creature takes a -2 penalty to AC until the start of your next turn.

features.name: Spell Thief
features.description: When a creature casts a spell that targets you or includes you in its area, you can use your reaction to make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check contested by the creature's Spellcasting Ability check. If you succeed, you negate the spell's effect against you and can cast the spell yourself.

features.name: Assassinate
features.description: You are an expert at infiltrating and striking at the perfect moment. If you hit a creature that is surprised with an attack on your first turn of combat, the attack is automatically a critical hit.

features.name: Infiltration Expertise
features.description: You can use your bonus action to gain advantage on your next Stealth check. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Impostor
features.description: You can use your action to adopt a humanoid's persona. You gain the creature's appearance and voice, and you can mimic its behavior. While you are impostor, you have advantage on Charisma (Deception) and Charisma (Performance) checks.

features.name: Death Strike
features.description: When you hit a creature with an attack and deal sneak attack damage to it, you can force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes an extra 10d6 poison damage.

features.name: Psychic Blades
features.description: You can manifest psionic blades that you can throw or use in melee. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can use your bonus action to deal an extra 1d6 psychic damage to the target.

features.name: Soul Blades
features.description: You can use your Psychic Blades a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Psychic Veil
features.description: You can use your action to become invisible until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Rend Mind
features.description: When you hit a creature with a weapon attack and deal sneak attack damage to it, you can force the creature to make an Intelligence saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes an extra 8d6 psychic damage.

features.name: Fast Hands
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can do one of the following. **Sleight of Hand.** Make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check to pick a lock or disarm a trap with Thieves' Tools or to pick a pocket. **Use an Object.** Take the Utilize action, or take the Magic action to use a magic item that requires that action.

features.name: Second-Story Work
features.description: You've trained to get into especially hard-to-reach places, granting you these benefits. **Climber.** You gain a Climb Speed equal to your Speed. **Jumper.** You can determine your jump distance using your Dexterity rather than your Strength.

features.name: Supreme Sneak
features.description: You gain the following Cunning Strike option. *Stealth Attack (Cost: 1d6).* If you have the Hide action's Invisible condition, this attack doesn't end that condition on you if you end the turn behind Three-Quarters Cover or Total Cover.

features.name: Use Magic Device
features.description: You've learned how to maximize use of magic items, granting you the following benefits. **Attunement.** You can attune to up to four magic items at once. **Charges.** Whenever you use a magic item property that expends charges, roll 1d6. On a roll of 6, you use the property without expending the charges. **Scrolls.** You can use any *Spell Scroll*, using Intelligence as your spellcasting ability for the spell. If the spell is a cantrip or a level 1 spell, you can cast it reliably. If the scroll contains a higher-level spell, you must first succeed on an Intelligence (Arcana) check (DC 10 plus the spell's level). On a successful check, you cast the spell from the scroll. On a failed check, the scroll disintegrates.

features.name: Thief's Reflexes
features.description: You are adept at laying ambushes and quickly escaping danger. You can take two turns during the first round of any combat. You take your first turn at your normal Initiative and your second turn at your Initiative minus 10.

features.name: Aberrant Origin
features.description: You choose the source of your aberrant magic: Alien Entity, Far Realm, Outer God, or Psionic Awakening. This choice grants you certain spells and abilities.

features.name: Psionic Spells
features.description: You learn additional spells based on your Aberrant Origin. These spells count as sorcerer spells for you.

features.name: Telekinetic Speech
features.description: You can cast the Telepathy spell without components. Once you do so, you can't cast it again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Reality Warp
features.description: You can use your action to warp reality in a 10-foot cube originating from a point you can see within 60 feet of you. Choose one of the following effects: Entropic Fate (force creatures to reroll attack rolls or saving throws), Warped Form (transform creatures into beastly forms).

features.name: Aberrant Apotheosis
features.description: You can use your action to undergo an aberrant transformation for 1 minute. For the duration, you gain the following benefits: you have a flying speed of 40 feet, you have resistance to acid, cold, fire, lightning, and thunder damage, and you can use your action to unleash a burst of psychic energy that deals 4d10 psychic damage to each creature within 30 feet of you.

features.name: Clockwork Magic
features.description: You learn additional spells based on the clockwork magic tradition. These spells count as sorcerer spells for you.

features.name: Restore Balance
features.description: You can use your action to restore balance to the magic around you. Choose one of the following effects: Fortify (grant a creature advantage on the next attack roll or saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn), Restore (end one condition or effect on a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you).

features.name: Bastion of Law
features.description: You and your allies within 30 feet of you have advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

features.name: Trance of Order
features.description: You can use your action to enter a trance of absolute order for 1 minute. For the duration, you have a +2 bonus to AC and saving throws, and attack rolls against you have disadvantage.

features.name: Clockwork Apotheosis
features.description: You can use your action to undergo a clockwork transformation for 1 minute. For the duration, you gain the following benefits: you have a +2 bonus to AC and saving throws, attack rolls against you have disadvantage, and at the start of each of your turns, you regain 1 sorcery point.

features.name: Draconic Resilience
features.description: The magic in your body manifests physical traits of your draconic gift. Your Hit Point maximum increases by 3, and it increases by 1 whenever you gain another Sorcerer level. Parts of you are also covered by dragon-like scales. While you aren't wearing armor, your base Armor Class equals 10 plus your Dexterity and Charisma modifiers.

features.name: Draconic Spells
features.description: When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Draconic Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. Table: Draconic Spells | Sorcerer Level    | Spells                                                 | |-------------------|--------------------------------------------------------| | 3                 | Alter Self, Chromatic Orb, Command, Dragon's Breath    | | 5                 | Fear, Fly                                              | | 7                 | Arcane Eye, Charm Monster                              | | 9                 | Legend Lore, Summon Dragon                             |

features.name: Elemental Affinity
features.description: Your draconic magic has an affinity with a damage type associated with dragons. Choose one of those types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. You have Resistance to that damage type, and when you cast a spell that deals damage of that type, you can add your Charisma modifier to one damage roll of that spell.

features.name: Dragon Wings
features.description: As a Bonus Action, you can cause draconic wings to appear on your back. The wings last for 1 hour or until you dismiss them (no action required). For the duration, you have a Fly Speed of 60 feet. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest unless you spend 3 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore your use of it.

features.name: Dragon Companion
features.description: You can cast *Summon Dragon* without a Material component. You can also cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a Long Rest. Whenever you start casting the spell, you can modify it so that it doesn't require Concentration. If you do so, the spell's duration becomes 1 minute for that casting.

features.name: Wild Magic Surge
features.description: Your spellcasting can unleash unpredictable magic. Immediately after you cast a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher, the DM can have you roll a d20. If you roll a 1, roll on the Wild Magic Surge table to create a magical effect.

features.name: Tides of Chaos
features.description: You can gain advantage on one attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. Once you do so, you must roll on the Wild Magic Surge table before you can use this feature again.

features.name: Bend Luck
features.description: You can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll or saving throw of a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Controlled Chaos
features.description: You can use your action to roll on the Wild Magic Surge table twice and choose which effect to use. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Spell Bombardment
features.description: When you roll damage for a spell, you can roll the spell's damage dice a second time and use the higher result. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Fey Presence
features.description: You can use your action to exude an aura of fey presence. Each creature within 10 feet of you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become charmed or frightened (your choice) for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

features.name: Misty Escape
features.description: When you take damage, you can use your reaction to turn invisible and teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space that you can see.

features.name: Beguiling Defenses
features.description: You are immune to being charmed, and when another creature attempts to charm you, you can use your reaction to attempt to turn the charm back on that creature. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

features.name: Dark Delirium
features.description: You can use your action to unleash fey madness. Each creature within 20 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is charmed or frightened (your choice) for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

features.name: Healing Light
features.description: You can use your action to touch a creature and restore hit points to it. The number of hit points restored is equal to a roll of your Warlock spell die + your Charisma modifier. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Radiant Soul
features.description: You gain resistance to radiant damage, and you can use your action to unleash a burst of radiant energy. Each creature within 10 feet of you must make a Constitution saving throw, taking 2d10 radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

features.name: Celestial Resilience
features.description: You gain temporary hit points equal to your Warlock level + your Charisma modifier at the start of each of your turns.

features.name: Searing Vengeance
features.description: When you or a creature within 30 feet of you drops to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to deal 4d10 radiant damage to each creature within 30 feet of you, and you can choose to restore hit points to the creature that dropped to 0 hit points equal to a roll of your Warlock spell die + your Charisma modifier.

features.name: Dark One's Blessing
features.description: When you reduce an enemy to 0 Hit Points, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Charisma modifier plus your Warlock level (minimum of 1 Temporary Hit Point). You also gain this benefit if someone else reduces an enemy within 10 feet of you to 0 Hit Points.

features.name: Fiend Spells
features.description: The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Warlock level specified in the Fiend Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared. Table: Fiend Spells | Warlock Level | Spells                                            | |---------------|---------------------------------------------------| | 3             | Burning Hands, Command, Scorching Ray, Suggestion | | 5             | Fireball, Stinking Cloud                          | | 7             | Fire Shield, Wall of Fire                         | | 9             | Geas, Insect Plague                               |

features.name: Dark One's Own Luck
features.description: You can call on your fiendish patron to alter fate in your favor. When you make an ability check or a saving throw, you can use this feature to add 1d10 to your roll. You can do so after seeing the roll but before any of the roll's effects occur. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), but you can use it no more than once per roll. You regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

features.name: Fiendish Resilience
features.description: Choose one damage type, other than Force, whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest. You have Resistance to that damage type until you choose a different one with this feature.

features.name: Hurl Through Hell
features.description: Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can try to instantly transport the target through the Lower Planes. The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC, or the target disappears and hurtles through a nightmare landscape. The target takes 8d10 Psychic damage if it isn't a Fiend, and it has the Incapacitated condition until the end of your next turn, when it returns to the space it previously occupied or the nearest unoccupied space. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest unless you expend a Pact Magic spell slot (no action required) to restore your use of it.

features.name: Awakened Mind
features.description: You can use your action to speak telepathically to any creature that can understand a language within 30 feet of you. You don't need to share a language with the creature for it to understand your telepathic messages.

features.name: Entropic Ward
features.description: When a creature makes an attack roll against you, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on the attack roll. If the attack misses you, you can add a d6 to your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

features.name: Thought Shield
features.description: You are immune to telepathy, and when a creature attempts to read your thoughts or deal psychic damage to you, you can use your reaction to deal psychic damage to that creature equal to your Charisma modifier.

features.name: Create Thrall
features.description: You can use your action to touch a humanoid and attempt to turn it into your thrall. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for 1 hour or until it takes damage. While charmed in this way, the target is under your control.

features.name: Arcane Ward
features.description: You can weave magic around yourself for protection. When you cast an abjuration spell, you can simultaneously use a bit of the spell's magic to create a magical ward on yourself. The ward has hit points equal to twice your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier.

features.name: Projected Ward
features.description: When a creature that you can see within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can use your reaction to cause your Arcane Ward to absorb that damage. If this damage reduces the ward to 0 hit points, the warded creature takes any remaining damage.

features.name: Improved Abjuration
features.description: Your abjuration spells are more effective. When you cast an abjuration spell that requires a saving throw, you can add your Intelligence modifier to the spell save DC.

features.name: Spell Resistance
features.description: You have resistance to spell damage, and you have advantage on saving throws against spells.

features.name: Portent
features.description: You can glimpse the future. When you finish a long rest, roll two d20s and record the numbers rolled. You can use your Portent dice to replace any attack roll, ability check, or saving throw made by you or a creature that you can see.

features.name: The Third Eye
features.description: You gain the ability to see things as they truly are. You can use your action to gain one of the following benefits for 1 hour: Darkvision (see in darkness), Ethereal Sight (see into the Ethereal Plane), Greater Comprehension (read any language), See Invisibility (see invisible creatures and objects).

features.name: Expert Divination
features.description: When you cast a divination spell of 2nd level or higher, you regain one expended spell slot. The slot must be of a level lower than the spell you cast.

features.name: The Third Eye Improved
features.description: Your Third Eye feature now lasts for 8 hours, and you can use it a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Evocation Savant
features.description: Choose two Wizard spells from the Evocation school, each of which must be no higher than level 2, and add them to your spellbook for free. In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Evocation school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

features.name: Potent Cantrip
features.description: Your damaging cantrips affect even creatures that avoid the brunt of the effect. When you cast a cantrip at a creature and you miss with the attack roll or the target succeeds on a saving throw against the cantrip, the target takes half the cantrip's damage (if any) but suffers no additional effect from the cantrip.

features.name: Sculpt Spells
features.description: You can create pockets of relative safety within the effects of your evocations. When you cast an Evocation spell that affects other creatures that you can see, you can choose a number of them equal to 1 plus the spell's level. The chosen creatures automatically succeed on their saving throws against the spell, and they take no damage if they would normally take half damage on a successful save.

features.name: Empowered Evocation
features.description: Whenever you cast a Wizard spell from the Evocation school, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of that spell.

features.name: Overchannel
features.description: You can increase the power of your spells. When you cast a Wizard spell with a spell slot of levels 1–5 that deals damage, you can deal maximum damage with that spell on the turn you cast it. The first time you do so, you suffer no adverse effect. If you use this feature again before you finish a Long Rest, you take 2d12 Necrotic damage for each level of the spell slot immediately after you cast it. This damage ignores Resistance and Immunity. Each time you use this feature again before finishing a Long Rest, the Necrotic damage per spell level increases by 1d12.

features.name: Improved Minor Illusion
features.description: You learn the Minor Illusion cantrip. If you already know this cantrip, you can learn a different wizard cantrip of your choice. The cantrip doesn't count against your number of cantrips known.

features.name: Malleable Illusions
features.description: When you cast an illusion spell that has a duration of 1 minute or longer, you can use your action to change the nature of the illusion (as if you had cast it with the new parameters).

features.name: Illusory Self
features.description: You can create an illusory duplicate of yourself as a reaction when you are hit by an attack. The attack misses you, and the illusion is destroyed. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

features.name: Illusory Reality
features.description: You can use your action to make one object or creature that is part of an illusion you created become real for 1 minute. The object or creature remains real for the duration, and it behaves as if it were a normal object or creature of its kind.
